mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically elevating equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market, marked by a sustained rise in the unemployment rate, signals impending economic contraction and reduced corporate profit growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances following a 0.3pp unemployment rise, or out-of-sample hit rate < 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in consumer retail spending and residential construction activity confirms broad-based domestic demand strength, supporting higher corporate revenue forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index reflects weakening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply chain bottlenecks, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth consistently coincides with positive 5-day forward returns in >50% of observations, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when CPIAUCSL exceeds 270.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate amid elevated inflation forces central banks to prioritize price stability over growth, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in >65% of months following a 0.25pp rate hike, or the interaction term between rate changes and inflation is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a resilient labor market and sustained consumer spending power, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a hit rate above 52%, or rolling out-of-sample R-squared remains below 0.05 over any 4-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the 2.0% threshold confirms robust macroeconomic expansion and corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.0% is followed by negative 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or the signal exhibits negative predictive accuracy during post-2020 recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized expansion in industrial production and retail sales eliminates supply-demand mismatches, stabilizing input costs and boosting near-term corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint positive movement of INDPRO and RSAFS yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in a 5-day return regression, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark in >40% of triggered weeks.
